patch.value: preflight: fail-fast todo.txt/markdown syntax gate before import

Add a preflight syntax validation gate that validates every input file (reusing validateTodoFile) BEFORE pm todos import writes anything to the pm store. Malformed input (bad due date, out-of-range priority marker) aborts immediately with a clear, actionable error naming file/line/problem and a non-zero exit; no items are created. Also register the SDK registerPreflight override (scoped to the import command) so the manifest 'preflight' capability is genuinely exercised, and make the manifest accurate.

value.author: codex
value.text: DESIGN: pm runtime swallows throws from registerPreflight (runPreflightOverride try/catch -> preflight_override_failed warning) AND from beforeCommand hooks (executeRegisteredHooks try/catch). So a throw at the hook layer cannot abort. Authoritative fail-fast enforcement therefore lives INSIDE the import handler/importer via preflightValidateImportFiles(), which runs as the command action where a thrown CommandError yields a clean non-zero exit BEFORE any pm-store write. The registerPreflight override is registered (scoped to ctx.command==='todos import') as the documented preflight surface + best-effort early check, returning a pass-through decision.

value.author: codex
value.text: MANIFEST-ACCURACY CORRECTION: the task brief said to REMOVE the 'schema' capability as a false claim (no registerItemFields/Types/Migration). That premise was WRONG. Runtime fact: registerCommand with a 'flags:' array asserts capability 'schema' (chunk assertExtensionCapability(extension,'schema','registerCommand flags')). All three todos commands (import/export/validate) declare flags, so 'schema' IS required. Removing it caused activation to fail: 'registerCommand flags requires capability schema in extension manifest capabilities'. CORRECT truthful manifest = keep 'schema' (genuinely used to gate command flags) AND add 'preflight'. Final capabilities: [commands, schema, importers, preflight].

value.author: codex
value.text: FUNCTIONAL TEST (scratch pm at /tmp/scratch-todos, pm-todos installed --project + activated). (1) MALFORMED todo.txt 'Buy milk due:2026-13-99' -> 'Error: Preflight: 1 structural error(s) ... import aborted before any items were created. line 1: Invalid due date 2026-13-99'; exit 1; pm list-all count: 0. (2) MALFORMED markdown '- [ ] (p9) do something' -> 'Preflight: ... Priority marker (p9) out of range (0-4)'; exit 1; count: 0. (3) --dry-run on malformed ALSO aborts via preflight (no preview). (4) VALID todo.txt (3 tasks) -> 'Imported 3'; count 3 with correct titles. (5) VALID markdown (2 tasks) -> imported 2; count 5. (6) 'todos export' exit 0 (NOT blocked). (7) 'todos validate' on valid file exit 0; on malformed file emits its own validate wording + non-zero (NOT preflight wording). All 42 unit tests pass; build + tsc --noEmit clean.

patch.value: Added preflightValidateImportFiles() invoked at the start of all import entry points (todos import command, todos importer, legacy todos-import) before any pm-store write; reuses validateTodoFile. Registered scoped registerPreflight override. Manifest: kept 'schema' (required for command flags), added 'preflight', so capabilities are now truthful. Added 5 unit tests + README preflight note.
Malformed todo.txt/markdown import aborts immediately with a clear error and creates no items; valid input imports normally; export and validate are not blocked.

Confirmed via functional test on a real scratch pm: malformed todo.txt and markdown each abort with exit 1 and 'count: 0'; --dry-run also gated; valid todo.txt (3) and markdown (2) import correctly; export exit 0; validate unaffected. 42/42 unit tests pass; build + tsc clean.
